description: HP 8901B Modulation Analyzer (RF frequency, AM/FM/PM modulation, audio, and RF power; 150 kHz to 1300 MHz). Legacy HP-IB (IEEE-488) instrument that uses two-character alphameric program codes (e.g. M1 = AM, M5 = FREQ), NOT SCPI. Codes are either two alpha characters or an alpha character plus a number (e.g. M3, S5, 27.3SP). Lower-case alpha characters are treated as upper case; commas and spaces between codes are ignored. Special functions are entered as the special-function number followed by the suffix SP (e.g. 7.4SP). Three syntax forms exist: a two-character code/suffix alone, a number plus suffix (e.g. 100 MZ), or a prefix special function plus number plus suffix (e.g. 27.3SP 100 MZ). Numeric data is a signed mantissa of up to eight digits with one decimal point and an optional one/two digit signed exponent. Measurement results are read back as an implicit-point exponential constant in fundamental units (Hz, watts, radians, dB, %) terminated by CR LF. Factory HP-IB address is decimal 14.

identity.description: HP-IB Identify. Distinguishes an HP 8901B from an HP 8902A. Sent as a query: the code ID is followed by reading the instrument, which outputs a numeric value identifying the instrument type.

commands.name: measure_am
commands.description: Select AM (amplitude modulation depth) measurement mode. Uses the receiver section. Read the result back from the bus after triggering.

commands.name: measure_fm
commands.description: Select FM (frequency modulation peak deviation) measurement mode. Uses the receiver section.

commands.name: measure_pm
commands.description: Select PM (phase modulation, also shown as PhiM) measurement mode. Uses the receiver section. Result is in radians.

commands.name: measure_rf_power
commands.description: Select RF Power measurement mode using an external power sensor on the SENSOR connector.

commands.name: measure_frequency
commands.description: Select RF (input) Frequency counter measurement mode. Uses the receiver section.

commands.name: measure_audio_freq
commands.description: Select Audio Frequency measurement mode. Use A1 first to measure the internally demodulated signal, or A2/AUDIO INPUT to measure an external audio signal.

commands.name: measure_audio_distortion
commands.description: Select Audio Distortion measurement mode. Use with D5 (1 kHz DISTN) or D6 (400 Hz DISTN) to set the distortion test frequency.

commands.name: measure_if_level
commands.description: Select IF Level measurement mode. Uses the receiver section.

commands.name: measure_tuned_rf_level
commands.description: Select Tuned RF Level measurement mode (uses the IF peak detector).

commands.name: measure_freq_error
commands.description: Select RF Frequency Error measurement mode.

commands.name: detector_peak_plus
commands.description: Select the Peak+ (positive peak) audio/modulation detector.

commands.name: detector_peak_minus
commands.description: Select the Peak- (negative peak) audio/modulation detector.

commands.name: detector_peak_hold
commands.description: Select the Peak Hold detector.

commands.name: detector_average
commands.description: Select the Average detector (RMS calibrated).

commands.name: distortion_1khz
commands.description: Select 1 kHz DISTN: make audio distortion/SINAD measurements on a 1 kHz audio signal.

commands.name: distortion_400hz
commands.description: Select 400 Hz DISTN: make audio distortion/SINAD measurements on a 400 Hz audio signal.

commands.name: detector_rms
commands.description: Select the RMS detector.

commands.name: detector_peak_half
commands.description: Select the Peak+-/2 detector (half the peak-to-peak value).

commands.name: display_log
commands.description: Display the measurement result in logarithmic units (dB). Used with R1 for dB Ratio.

commands.name: display_lin
commands.description: Display the measurement result in linear units. Used with R1 for percent Ratio.

commands.name: ext_atten_off
commands.description: Turn dB EXT ATTEN (external attenuation/gain offset) off.

commands.name: ext_atten_on
commands.description: Turn dB EXT ATTEN on, offsetting level measurements by an external attenuation or gain value. Precede with a numeric value to enter the offset (e.g. nn N1).

commands.name: ratio_off
commands.description: Turn RATIO off.

commands.name: ratio_on
commands.description: Turn RATIO on: compare the current measurement to a stored reference. Precede with a numeric value to enter the ratio reference (e.g. nnn R1). Use LN for percent ratio or LG for dB ratio.

commands.name: previous_ratio
commands.description: PREVIOUS RATIO: compare to the previously established ratio reference.

commands.name: hp_filter_off
commands.description: Turn all HP (High-Pass) filters off.

commands.name: hp_filter_50hz
commands.description: Turn the 50 Hz high-pass filter on.

commands.name: hp_filter_300hz
commands.description: Turn the 300 Hz high-pass filter on.

commands.name: lp_filter_off
commands.description: Turn all LP (Low-Pass) filters off.

commands.name: lp_filter_3khz
commands.description: Turn the 3 kHz low-pass filter on.

commands.name: lp_filter_15khz
commands.description: Turn the 15 kHz low-pass filter on.

commands.name: lp_filter_20khz
commands.description: Turn the >20 kHz low-pass filter on.

commands.name: deemphasis_off
commands.description: Pre-display off and FM De-emphasis off.

commands.name: deemphasis_on
commands.description: Pre-display on and FM De-emphasis on (measure FM deviation after de-emphasis).

commands.name: deemphasis_25us
commands.description: Select the 25 microsecond FM de-emphasis network.

commands.name: deemphasis_50us
commands.description: Select the 50 microsecond FM de-emphasis network.

commands.name: deemphasis_75us
commands.description: Select the 75 microsecond FM de-emphasis network.

commands.name: deemphasis_750us
commands.description: Select the 750 microsecond FM de-emphasis network.

commands.name: calibrate_off
commands.description: CALIBRATE off.

commands.name: calibrate_on
commands.description: CALIBRATE on: initiate AM, FM, or RF Power calibration.

commands.name: cal_factor
commands.description: Percent CAL FACTOR. With no preceding value, reads back the current calibration factor for the selected measurement. With a preceding numeric value, enters a calibration factor (in percent).

commands.name: zero
commands.description: ZERO: cancel any dc offset present in the power sensor (RF Power measurement).

commands.name: save_cal
commands.description: SAVE CAL: save the resulting calibration factor for RF Power, AM, or FM.

commands.name: auto_tuning
commands.description: AUTO TUNING: enable automatic tuning to the input signal. Combine with K0 (lock mode) or K1 (track mode).

commands.name: input_frequency_mhz
commands.description: MHz: complete keyboard entry of an input (manual tuning) frequency in MHz. Used alone (MZ) it centers the IF; preceded by a value it tunes to that frequency.

commands.name: input_frequency_hz
commands.description: Hz: complete keyboard entry of an input (manual tuning) frequency in Hz.

commands.name: increment_up_hz
commands.description: Increment up by the Hz increment step size (up arrow Hz).

commands.name: increment_down_hz
commands.description: Increment down by the Hz increment step size (down arrow Hz).

commands.name: increment_up_khz
commands.description: Increment up by the kHz increment step size (up arrow kHz). Precede with a value to set the kHz step size.

commands.name: increment_down_khz
commands.description: Increment down by the kHz increment step size (down arrow kHz).

commands.name: track_mode_off
commands.description: TRACK Mode off (low-noise lock mode).

commands.name: track_mode_on
commands.description: TRACK Mode on: track drifting input signals from 10 MHz to 1300 MHz.

commands.name: display_freq
commands.description: DISPLAY FREQ: display (read back) the RF frequency the instrument last tuned to.

commands.name: display_increment
commands.description: DISPLAY INCREMENT: display (read back) the frequency increment step size.

commands.name: store
commands.description: STORE: store the current measurement state (up to eight states, 0-7). Precede with a register number.

commands.name: recall
commands.description: RECALL: recall a stored measurement state (0-7). Precede with a register number.

commands.name: special_function
commands.description: SPECIAL FUNCTION suffix. A special function code is formed by the special-function number (prefix.suffix) followed by SP. With no preceding number, SP reads back which operator-selected special functions (1-10) are set.

parameters.name: function
parameters.range: 1 to 99 (1-10 instrument circuitry, 11-37 measurement modes, 40-99 service)

commands.name: special_special
commands.description: SPECIAL, SPECIAL: reads back the instrument-selected (currently in-use) special function settings (1-10).

commands.name: units_mv
commands.description: Select millivolt (mV) units for level measurements.

commands.name: units_uv
commands.description: Select microvolt (uV) units for level measurements.

commands.name: units_volts
commands.description: Select Volt (V) units for level measurements.

commands.name: units_watts
commands.description: Select Watt (W) units for level measurements.

commands.name: modulation_output
commands.description: Select MODULATION OUTPUT: process the internally demodulated signal (audio input off).

commands.name: audio_input
commands.description: Select AUDIO INPUT: process an external audio signal applied to the MODULATION OUTPUT/AUDIO INPUT connector. (Table 3-13 also shows A2 used to select external audio measurements.)

commands.name: automatic_operation
commands.description: AUTOMATIC OPERATION: automatically tune and autorange to make the selected measurement. Should appear at the beginning of a programming string. Resets special functions.

commands.name: auto_ranging
commands.description: Auto-Ranging on (RANGE HOLD off).

commands.name: range_hold
commands.description: RANGE HOLD: freeze the current ranges in use.

commands.name: enable_errors
commands.description: ENABLE ERRORS: re-enable Error 01 through Error 04.

commands.name: disable_errors
commands.description: DISABLE ERRORS: disable Error 01 through Error 04.

commands.name: clear
commands.description: CLEAR (key). Clears the current entry; for a volatile measurement mode, returns the instrument to the last selected non-volatile measurement mode. Also acts as a measurement trigger in Trigger Hold mode.

commands.name: instrument_preset
commands.description: INSTR PRESET: configure the instrument to its power-up condition (same effect as the DCL device clear message). Sets Free-run trigger (T0).

commands.name: identify
commands.description: HP-IB Identify. Distinguishes an HP 8901B from an HP 8902A. Read the instrument after sending ID to obtain the identifying value.

commands.name: trigger_off
commands.description: Trigger Off / Free-Run: measurements are taken continuously and updated as rapidly as settings can change. Default after DCL, entering remote, or IP.

commands.name: trigger_hold
commands.description: Trigger Hold: inhibit output to the display and bus until a trigger (T2, T3, GET, or CLEAR) is received.

commands.name: trigger_immediate
commands.description: Trigger Immediate: make one measurement and display the result without a settling time delay, then enter Trigger Hold.

commands.name: trigger_with_settling
commands.description: Trigger With Settling: make a settled measurement (equivalent to the bus command GET), then enter Trigger Hold.

commands.name: hex_a
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit A (used when entering hexadecimal values for certain special functions).

commands.name: hex_b
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit B.

commands.name: hex_c
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit C.

commands.name: hex_d
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit D.

commands.name: hex_e
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit E.

commands.name: hex_f
commands.description: Hexadecimal digit F.

commands.name: sf_fm_calibrator
commands.description: FM Calibration Actual: enable the internal FM calibrator. 12.1SP = FM Residual Calibration, 12.2SP = FM Peak Calibration.

commands.name: sf_am_calibrator
commands.description: AM Calibration Actual: enable the internal AM calibrator. 13.1SP = AM Residual Calibration, 13.2SP = AM Peak Residual Calibration.

commands.name: sf_read_lower_limit
commands.description: Read the lower limit value. Related limit special functions: 14.6SP read upper limit, 14.7SP read lower limit measurement code, 14.8SP read upper limit measurement code, 14.0SP disable limits, 14.1SP/14.2SP enter limit.

commands.name: sf_timebase_oven
commands.description: Timebase Oven Hot/Cold check. Returns 0 if the Option 002 reference oven is adequately warm (or not installed); Error 12 if cold.

commands.name: sf_timebase_source
commands.description: Timebase Internal/External. Returns 1 if an external time base is in use, 0 if the internal time base is in use.

commands.name: sf_read_am_cal_factor
commands.description: Read the AM Calibration Factor. 16.0SP disable, 16.1SP re-enable.

commands.name: sf_read_fm_cal_factor
commands.description: Read the FM Calibration Factors. 17.0SP disable, 17.1SP re-enable.

commands.name: sf_squelched_tone_receiver
commands.description: Squelched Tone Receiver mode (Other Modulation Capability). Shown as 18.XSP in the manual.

commands.name: sf_read_hpib_address
commands.description: Read the HP-IB address (Reading Internal User Information). Shown as 21.XSP in the manual.

commands.name: sf_srq_mask
commands.description: SRQ Mask (Special Function 22): sets which Status Byte conditions generate a Service Request. 22.0SP is the default/cleared mask state set by a Clear message. 22.64SP displays (reads back) the SRQ Mask. A numeric weight may precede 22 to set the mask.

commands.name: sf_read_ext_atten
commands.description: Read the dB External Attenuation value. 25.0SP disable, enter value with nn N1.

commands.name: sf_frequency_offset
commands.description: Frequency Offset Mode: enter and enable an external LO frequency. 27.0SP disable, 27.1SP enable, 27.2SP display external LO frequency. Enter value as 27.3SP nn MZ.

commands.name: sf_sinad
commands.description: Audio SINAD measurement (at 1 kHz or 400 Hz). Use A2 to select an external audio signal. Shown as 29.XSP in the manual.

commands.name: sf_audio_rms_level
commands.description: External Audio RMS Level measurement (rms voltage of an external audio signal at the AUDIO INPUT port). Shown as 30.XSP in the manual; select voltage units with UV, MV, or VL.

commands.name: sf_lo_frequency
commands.description: LO Frequency measurement. Shown as 33.XSP in the manual.

commands.name: sf_if_frequency
commands.description: IF Frequency measurement. Shown as 34.XSP in the manual.

commands.name: sf_peak_rf_level
commands.description: Peak RF Level measurement (RF Peak detector). Shown as 35.XSP in the manual.

commands.name: sf_rf_power_cal_factor_status
commands.description: RF Power Calibration Factor status. 37.0SP automatic, 37.1SP manual, 37.4SP RF Power calibration factor table size, 37.3-37.6/37.9SP edit calibration factor tables. Enter automatic CF as 37.3SP nn CF.

commands.name: sf_software_date_code
commands.description: Read the firmware Software Date Code. Shown as 42.XSP in the manual.

commands.name: sf_resolution_10hz
commands.description: Select 10 Hz frequency counter resolution.

commands.name: sf_resolution_1khz
commands.description: Select 1000 Hz frequency counter resolution.

commands.name: sf_resolution_1hz
commands.description: Select 1 Hz frequency counter resolution (not available on all instruments; returns Error 23 if unequipped).
